ctx2skill_evidence.selected_reason: The candidate 'check_pdf_fillable_fields' directly encapsulates the core functionality of the provided script: determining whether a PDF has fillable form fields. It is atomic, reusable, and maps cleanly to the challenge tasks. The cross-time replay lite score of 0.85 reflects high confidence that this skill can be applied across different PDF-checking scenarios with minimal adaptation.

creation_context.raw_content_preview:
# Source: tasks/court-form-filling/environment/skills/pdf/scripts/check_fillable_fields.py
# Project: SkillsBench

import sys

from pypdf import PdfReader

# Script for Claude to run to determine whether a PDF has fillable form fields. See forms.md.


reader = PdfReader(sys.argv[1])
if reader.get_fields():
    print("This PDF has fillable form fields")
else:
    print("This PDF does not have fillable form fields; you will need to visually determine where to enter data")
